brief_justification: Arrives at the correct answer (Yes, 2,000 > 1,608) with full closed-form derivation and independently catches the erroneous 27,000-tree figure in the problem statement; traces equilibrium points (N*=1,600 vs N*=800), explains the death-spiral mechanism, and links it to autopoietic loop integrity — clearly multi-level; explicitly re-derives from first principles, flags the 27,000 discrepancy mid-analysis, and investigates its probable cause, demonstrating active process monitoring; builds closed-form solutions, tabulates Company B's population, confirms both companies' totals with ✓ marks, and applies a sanity check against unconstrained growth — full verification suite; states the model assumption upfront (grow-then-cut recurrence), clearly separates correct conclusions from the erroneous 27,000 claim, and offers plausible causes without overstating certainty; considers only the direct mathematical approach with no comparison to alternative modeling conventions or interpretations of the harvest model (e.g., cut-then-grow ordering); every formula, equilibrium calculation, time-to-depletion derivation, and tabulated value is shown with explicit steps — fully reproducible; the response correctly models resource limits and depletion dynamics, inherently avoiding the 'assume unlimited resource' failure pattern, but never names or addresses the negative gate anti-pattern explicitly; suppressed modes — resource infinity assumption, first-order-only, local optimization bias, single hypothesis commitment — are all absent from the response, and the 50-year horizon framing explicitly counters local optimization; the injection topology specifies a game-theoretic commons path (Nash equilibria, mechanism design, enforceability gates, structural/social mechanism fork) that the agent entirely ignores, treating the task as a pure arithmetic verification.
